1. `rotate`: Initiates the rotation at a specific node `?n` with a rotation `?r`. It changes the heading of `?n` and sets up the first pass cursor `node-first-pass-next`.
2. `rotate-first-pass`: Iterates through the string starting from the node after `?n`. It updates the heading and position of nodes in the string based on the rotation. It moves the cursor `node-first-pass-next` down the string.
3. `rotate-first-pass-end`: Marks the end of the first pass when the last node is reached. It sets up the second pass cursor `node-second-pass-next`.
4. `rotate-second-pass`: Iterates through the string again to set the final positions of the nodes. It moves the cursor `node-second-pass-next` down the string.
5. `rotate-second-pass-end`: Marks the end of the second pass and clears the `rotating` flag.
